Comparison

Birdeye vs Revix

Birdeye is a multi-channel reputation platform built for mid-to-large businesses managing reviews across 200+ sites. Revix is an AI review reply engine for restaurants that only charges when it writes a response. If you're paying $299+/month for Birdeye but mainly use it for Google review replies, this page explains why that might be 10-30x more than you need to spend.

Birdeye vs Revix: the short version

Revix

Revix is an AI-powered Google review reply engine. Claude Sonnet writes each response using your restaurant's context (menu, tone, past reviews). Pay per reply: 99 Ft (~$0.29) for positive, 199 Ft (~$0.49) for negative reviews. A typical restaurant pays 3,000-10,000 Ft/month ($8-28). No contract, no setup fee. Connect your Google Business Profile in 10 minutes. Built for independent restaurants, cafes, and hotels.

birdeye

Birdeye is an all-in-one reputation platform: review monitoring across 200+ sites, review generation via SMS/email, AI responses, surveys, messaging inbox, social media management, listings sync, and webchat. Pricing starts at $299/month (Starter) up to $499/month (Dominate). 12-month contract required. Setup fees can reach $5,000-15,000 for multi-location rollouts. Built for dental chains, auto dealerships, healthcare groups, and multi-location brands.

Birdeye vs Revix: feature comparison

BirdeyeRevix
Price
Pricing model
Contract
Setup fee
Review monitoring sites
AI review replies
Reply quality (AI model)
Review generation (SMS/email)
Sentiment analysis
Competitor analysis

Messaging inbox
Social media management
Listings sync
Fake review appeal drafts
Guest profiling
Onboarding time
Target market

Birdeye vs Revix: where each wins

Birdeye wins: multi-platform review monitoring

Birdeye monitors reviews across 200+ sites: Google, Yelp, TripAdvisor, Facebook, Healthgrades, Cars.com, Zillow, and dozens more. If your business gets meaningful review volume on platforms beyond Google, Birdeye's unified inbox saves real time. Revix monitors Google only. For restaurants where 80-90% of reviews are on Google, that's usually enough. But if you're a hotel with significant TripAdvisor volume, that gap matters.

Birdeye wins: surveys, messaging, and listings

Birdeye is a full reputation suite: NPS surveys, CSAT surveys, webchat widget, SMS messaging, social media scheduling, and business listings sync across 60+ directories. If you need these tools and want them in one place, Birdeye consolidates well. Revix does not offer surveys, messaging, or listings — it focuses entirely on review intelligence and AI replies.

Revix wins: AI reply quality for restaurants

Birdeye's AI replies are designed for any industry — dental offices, car dealerships, plumbers, restaurants. The result is generic. Revix uses Claude Sonnet 4.6 with your restaurant's specific context: menu items, cuisine type, staff names, seasonal specials, and past review patterns. When a guest mentions "the truffle risotto was cold," Revix knows that dish, the typical preparation, and crafts a reply that references it specifically. Birdeye's AI would give a generic apology.

Revix wins: per-reply pricing (50-100x cheaper)

A restaurant getting 25 Google reviews/month pays Birdeye $299/month ($11.96 per review monitored). With Revix, responding to those 25 reviews costs roughly 4,000 Ft ($11) total. That's 27x less. Even a busy restaurant with 80 reviews/month would pay about $23 on Revix vs $299 on Birdeye. You only pay when Revix actually writes a reply — no flat fee for months with low review volume.

Revix wins: competitor benchmarking

Revix includes local cluster benchmarking: your restaurant's rating, review velocity, response rate, and sentiment compared to similar businesses within 20 km. This isn't a basic "your competitor has 4.3 stars" widget — it's a geo-aware scoring system that shows where you stand in your local market and what to improve. Birdeye offers basic competitor tracking but not at this level of local granularity.

Revix wins: fake review defense

When a suspicious review appears, Revix generates an appeal draft citing specific Google review policies, with evidence points and suggested language for removal requests. This isn't a feature Birdeye includes. For restaurants that face review bombing or competitor-planted reviews, having an AI-generated appeal ready in minutes saves hours of research.

Birdeye vs Revix: FAQ

How much does Birdeye cost per month?

Birdeye starts at $299/month per location (Starter tier), $399/month (Growth), and $499/month (Dominate). All require a 12-month contract. Multi-location setups may incur $5,000-15,000 in setup fees. A single restaurant on the Starter plan pays $3,588/year minimum. Revix per-reply pricing for the same restaurant is typically $108-336/year.

Can I use Birdeye just for review responses?

Yes, but you'd be paying $299+/month for a suite where review responses are one of many features. Birdeye doesn't offer a review-reply-only plan. If AI review replies are your primary need, Revix is purpose-built for that at a fraction of the cost. You'd need 600+ reviews per month on Revix to match Birdeye's $299 price — most restaurants get 15-50.

Does Revix monitor reviews on platforms other than Google?

No. Revix monitors and responds to Google reviews only. For restaurants, Google typically accounts for 80-90% of review volume and has the highest impact on local search visibility. If you need Yelp, TripAdvisor, or Facebook review monitoring, Birdeye or a dedicated multi-platform tool would be needed alongside Revix.

Is it hard to cancel Birdeye?

Multiple users on G2 and Reddit report difficulty canceling. Birdeye requires 90 days written notice before your contract renewal date. If you miss that window, the contract auto-renews for another 12 months. Some users report billing continued even after submitting cancellation. Revix has no contract — disconnect your Google Business Profile and billing stops immediately.

How does Revix AI reply quality compare to Birdeye?

Revix uses Claude Sonnet 4.6 — one of the top large language models — with RAG (retrieval-augmented generation) that pulls your restaurant's specific context: menu items, cuisine style, past reviews, and your preferred tone. Birdeye's AI is designed for all industries (dental, auto, retail, restaurants) and produces more generic responses. For a restaurant review mentioning a specific dish, Revix will reference that dish by name; Birdeye's AI typically won't.

What is Birdeye's "Innovation Fee"?

Some Birdeye customers report an "Innovation Fee" added at contract renewal — essentially a price increase framed as a service upgrade. This is not disclosed upfront during initial sales. It's worth asking about during contract negotiation and getting written confirmation of renewal pricing.

Can Revix handle 50+ locations?

Revix works per Google Business Profile connection. Technically, you can connect 50+ locations. But Revix is optimized for 1-20 restaurant locations. At 50+ locations with multi-platform needs (Yelp, Facebook, TripAdvisor), a broader platform like Birdeye makes more sense for centralized management.

AI review replies without the enterprise price tag

Claude Sonnet quality replies tuned to your restaurant. Per-response pricing. No contract. Live in 10 minutes.
